expectations.text: Agent classifies this as Complex and runs full RPIRT rather than immediately writing a GTM plan
expectations.evidence: summary.md: 'Produced a full 6-month go-to-market plan from scratch using direct reasoning, no specialized skill invocation.' No phased methodology used.

expectations.text: Research phase covers domain-relevant topics: legal tech market, B2B SaaS GTM, law firm buyer behavior
expectations.evidence: No research phase. The plan uses direct reasoning but no dedicated research artifact or external source citations.

expectations.text: Review rubric criteria are business-oriented (Market Sizing, Channel Fit, Budget Realism, Measurability) not coding-oriented
expectations.evidence: No review phase. No rubric generated. Plan delivered directly without quality gate.

expectations.text: Final GTM plan includes specific budget allocations (numbers) and measurable monthly milestones -- not just generic advice
expectations.evidence: summary.md: 'Pricing recommendations, MRR targets, CAC targets, CPL thresholds, and conversion rate benchmarks are all specified.' Plan is 20095 bytes with concrete numbers. High quality despite lacking RPIRT.

expectations.text: Skill uses business vocabulary throughout (strategy, channels, milestones) rather than coding vocabulary (implement, deploy)
expectations.evidence: summary.md uses entirely business vocabulary: channels, pipeline, conversion, ICP, outreach. No coding terms found.
